reflection:
your whoop skin temp climbed from 33.7 to 34.9 over two nights. that is a real signal, not just "feels like a fever." so when you wrote "no multivitamin, let my body ride the freaking storm," you are right to strip out the noise while you figure out what is going on. riding the storm doesn't mean ignoring it though. eat, hydrate, and watch the dizziness, if it keeps showing up on flat ground that is a different conversation.

second night in a row you woke at 4 and couldn't fall back. last meal 19:05, full wind-down ticked, target bedtime hit. it isn't a behavioral cascade this time, it is probably the low fever doing the waking. notice it, don't catastrophize it.

mood 5, energy 5, but coccyx pain has come down from 4 to 3 over the week and pelvic floor from 5 to 3. quiet win, log it. and your gratitude entries today were honest in a good way, the AI helping you read your book, cosita, the fact that you woke up hard. those are not small things to write down at 5am.

dinner with filip and nasti, ping-pong with laura with the brain working better than expected, duolingo easier somehow. that is a body fighting something and a person still showing up.

one thing for today, open the team speech doc and write the first 200 words before you check anything else. you are worried about it, the worry will keep eating at the background until you start.
opener: skin temp jumped 1°C, body's working. write the speech first.
focus_for_today: open the team-speech doc, write the first 200 words before anything else
